Never in my wildest dreams did I think I would get a chance to play for the club I supported as a little kid. When I was approached by Arsenal in 2005 my world changed forever. I began my journey as a 16 year old boy at the Arsenal's academy, trying to one day become a first team player, going out for evening runs in East Barnet with Jack Wilshere. Little did we know then, that couple years later we'd beat Barcelona in the Champions League and wear the no.1 and no.10 shirts.
It has been over 11 years since I became an Arsenal player and I never thought the day I leave this club would come. Everything that I am and everything that I have, I owe to Arsenal, Arsene Wenger, Liam Brady, David Court, Bob Arber, Steve Bould, Neil Banfield, Mike Salmon, Tony Roberts, Gerry Peyton, Pat Rice and many others. Today I move on, ready for the new challenge in my life, taking with me only the good memories and bag of expierience. I leave hopeful that those days of Berkamp,Henry, Seaman and others are soon to return to Emirates Stadium and Arsenal get back to winning the Premier League.
I may not be an Arsenal player no more but I will carry the Arsenal name with me wherever I go and I will do so with great pride.
Once a Gunner, always a Gunner! ❤

I remember being a young boy in Poland, watching David Seaman, Thierry Henry, Dennis Berkamp and others play for Arsenal. Never in my wildest dreams did I think I would get a chance to play for the club I supported as a little kid. When I was approached by Arsenal in 2005 my world changed forever. I began my journey as a 16 year old boy at the Arsenal's academy, trying to one day become a first team player, going out for evening runs in East Barnet with Jack Wilshere. Little did we know then, that couple years later we'd beat Barcelona in the Champions League and wear the no.1 and no.10 shirts.
It has been over 11 years since I became an Arsenal player and I never thought the day I leave this club would come. Everything that I am and everything that I have, I owe to Arsenal, Arsene Wenger, Liam Brady, David Court, Bob Arber, Steve Bould, Neil Banfield, Mike Salmon, Tony Roberts, Gerry Peyton, Pat Rice and many others. Words can't describe my gratitude to these people and love for this club.
Today I move on, ready for the new challenge in my life, taking with me only the good memories and bag of expierience. I leave hopeful that those days of Berkamp,Henry, Seaman and others are soon to return to Emirates Stadium and Arsenal get back to winning the Premier League.
I may not be an Arsenal player no more but I will carry the Arsenal name with me wherever I go and I will do so with great pride.
Once a Gunner, always a Gunner! ❤

Arsene Wenger has seen his pre-season preparations hit by a sickness bug.

Per Mertesacker and Olivier Giroud both missed the friendly with Bayern Munich , Theo Walcott and Aaron Ramsey played despite feeling ill while Sead Kolasinac was sick on the pitch.


The Arsenal boss Wenger has put it down to food poisoning and also revealed that his assistant Steve Bould has missed the Chinese leg of the trip after undergoing surgery on a hernia in his neck. Laurent Koscielny is also nursing a back injury.

Wenger, whose side beat Bayern Munich on penalties despite sweltering heat in Shanghai, said: "We had to dig deep and think we will benefit from it. The doctor thinks it is food poisoning.

"Kolasinac was sick as well. Walcott, Aaron, all had a little bit of food poisoning as well.

"Steve Bould has a hernia on his neck and had to do it in Australia and he couldn’t travel. He went home on Saturday. He’s OK. He went home and he’s good."

Wenger’s men did beat the Germans on penalties after Alex Iwobi’s last gasp leveller took it to spot kicks. Sub keeper Emiliano Martinez was the shoot out hero, saving two spot kicks while Petr Cech was man of the match after a string of brilliant saves before going off.

on Elneny playing at centre back…
He played at centre back because our centre backs, one was sick with food poisoning - Mertesacker, we had to send him back to the hotel - and Koscielny had a problem with his back so he was not available.

on Lacazette playing through the middle even when Alexis is back…
Lacazette will play centre forward and Giroud was sick as well and had to stay at the hotel. Alexis plays where he plays always, on the left, sometimes on the right but normally on the left.

on why he took Cech off…
Because I want to give a chance to the other goalkeepers so they have experience as well. I can only do it in preseason that I can take a goalkeeper off after 70 minutes. In fact the goalkeeper who came on played well so I think it was a good night for both goalkeepers.

on whether Cech will be first-choice goalkeeper…
They all fight for it but at the moment he is doing very well. Ospina will play against Chelsea on Saturday, and we will do the same against them - Ospina for 70 minutes and then 20 minutes for Martinez. Cech will rest on Saturday.

on the performances against Bayern last season…
I don’t think in any way that this kind of game wipes out what happened last season. We all remember that. We had a nightmare in the second halves [of those games] because if you take the results of the first games, we go through. It is different here, but it is still good to win a game against Bayern because it can help the team grow in confidence and to think that we can continue to prepare well in a good mood. We are now 10 or 11 days away so it helps to push on with the last step in our two weeks preparations, so on that front, mentally, it’s good.

on whether the team has a winning mentality…

得熱狗冇

“我記得當自己還是個孩子的時候，就在看施文、亨利、柏金以及其他阿仙奴球員的比賽，我從來不曾想到自己能夠有機會為這支兒童時就支持的球隊踢球。當2005年阿仙奴找到我時，我的世界就此發生改變。16歲時，我開始了在阿仙奴青訓營的生活，希望通過努力有朝一日能成為球隊的一號門將。我經常和韋舒亞在East Barnet夜跑，那時候我們不會想到，幾年之後我們會在歐冠中擊敗巴塞，並且分別穿上球隊的1號和10號球衣。”
“我成為一名阿仙奴球員已經11年了，我也從來沒有想過自己有朝一日會就此離開。我現在的樣子以及目前擁有的一切，都要歸功於阿仙奴、雲加以及很多很多人。我無法用語言表達對他們的感激以及對阿仙奴的愛。”
“今天就此別過，我會帶著那些美好的回憶和自己所有的經歷繼續前行，做好準備迎接新的挑戰。希望有朝一日，柏金、亨利時代的榮光會在我們的主場重現，我們會重新奪回英超的冠軍。”
“今後我可能不會再是一名阿仙奴球員了，但我無論走到何方，都會把‘槍手’這個名字帶在身上，我會因此感到驕傲和自豪。”
“一日槍手，終生槍手！”

“我記得當自己還是個孩子的時候，就在看施文、亨利、柏金以及其他阿仙奴球員的比賽，我從來不曾想到自己能夠有機會為這支兒童時就支持的球隊踢球。當2005年阿仙奴找到我時，我的世界就此發生改變。16歲時，我開始了在阿仙奴青訓營的生活，希望通過努力有朝一日能成為球隊的一號門將。我經常和韋舒亞在East Barnet夜跑，那時候我們不會想到，幾年之後我們會在歐冠中擊敗巴塞，並且分別穿上球隊的1號和10號球衣。”
“我成為一名阿仙奴球員已經11年了，我也從來沒有想過自己有朝一日會就此離開。我現在的樣子以及目前擁有的一切，都要歸功於阿仙奴、雲加以及很多很多人。我無法用語言表達對他們的感激以及對阿仙奴的愛。”
“今天就此別過，我會帶著那些美好的回憶和自己所有的經歷繼續前行，做好準備迎接新的挑戰。希望有朝一日，柏金、亨利時代的榮光會在我們的主場重現，我們會重新奪回英超的冠軍。”
“今後我可能不會再是一名阿仙奴球員了，但我無論走到何方，都會把‘槍手’這個名字帶在身上，我會因此感到驕傲和自豪。”
“一日槍手，終生槍手！”

干地語帶諷刺地道：「馬迪將會離去，而我是最後一個知道的人；一隊現在只有17名球員，卻將青訓新兵不斷外借，中場線只餘下簡迪和法比加斯，然後你叫我去衛冕聯賽冠軍和挑戰歐聯？哈哈，我想我亦下場踢球是唯一的辦法。」

記者問道為何球會不斷流失球員，卻無合適補充時。干地語帶怒氣回應：「5月時我已列出一份為數十人的球員名單給董事會引進，可是建議均石沉大海，那名女高層為首的轉會團隊對我的名單從來沒有任何回音。」

記者試探性地問道：「你是指球會女總裁簡奴夫斯佳亞嗎？」干地點頭並補充道：「你知道嗎？她干涉男人的工作只是因為她需要性；我下定了決心，今晚會去找該名女高層，然後以性滿足她，令她屈服。」


記者提醒干地別說笑，這種說話公開或會引起法律問題時，干地回應道：「你看看明天的報紙，如果車路士宣布買進莫拉達，就是我『屌柒佢』，令她有高潮的證明。」

記者有些佩服他的霸氣：「好吧，我會如實刊出這個報道，你想以甚麼標題命名呢？」

「嗯，『給我一晚時間，換車路士十個月安穩』吧。」

【now.com體育】阿仙奴領隊雲加承認大軍在周三對拜仁慕尼黑的國際冠軍盃，受食物中毒影響而打亂部署。

阿仙奴周三在上海舉行的國冠盃劣勢下逼和拜仁慕尼黑，最終更憑互射12碼打敗對手，連帶澳洲之旅已取得季前賽3連勝。不過領隊雲加賽後透露一個不為人知的事，那就是大軍賽前飽受食物中毒困擾。

雲加指前鋒基奧特受影響，還有梅迪薩卡因此在開賽前才退出比賽，而新加盟的哥拉辛拿斯亦因此半場就退下火線。

「我為這夜可能面對大難關感到有點兒害怕，事實上，上半場我們陷苦戰。藍斯完成比賽，但禾確特、哥拉辛拿斯、梅迪薩卡及基奧特都有點食物中毒。」不過雲加強調不清楚食物中毒原因。

阿仙奴完成此戰後，周六轉戰北京鬥倫敦死敵車路士。

阿仙奴完成此戰後，周六轉戰北京鬥倫敦死敵車路士。
